question.text: FAS 133 (US accounting standard) requires an additional qualification for derivatives beyond those in IAS 39. What is this additional requirement?

answer.text: Trade must settle (or be capable of being settled) on net basis and not on gross basis
Value of derivative must be linked to the value of underlying
Trade must be settled on a future date
On trade date, there should be no full cash outlay

explanation.text: **The Logic:** IAS 39 and AS 30 require 3 criteria: (1) value linked to underlying, (2) future settlement, (3) no full cash outlay on trade date. FAS 133 adds a 4th: the trade must settle (or be capable of being settled) on a net basis, not gross. **The Trap:** The other three options are the IAS 39/AS 30 criteria, not the additional FAS 133 requirement. **Cross-Exam Context:** Derivative definitions and accounting standards are also relevant to Series VIII and IV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This distinction matters for accounting classification — if a contract doesn't meet all criteria, it may not qualify as a derivative for accounting purposes.
